prompt:
This prospect looks like a peer — another AI/automation consultant. What do I send back?

Conversation:
[Me, Day 0 connection note]: Hey Steven, came across Rivet AI while exploring the automation space. The founder-led stage you're building through is one I find genuinely interesting to follow. Looking forward to being in your network.

[Steven, Day 1]: Appreciate it Aleem. Checked out your profile — looks like we're in overlapping territory. Always good to know other builders in the space. What are you focused on lately?

Profile: Steven Roberts, Founder of Rivet AI (solo consultancy, AI strategy and implementation for mid-market companies, based in Denver).
assertions: Message body (excluding meta-line) is under 1000 characters
Contains at least one Voss label
Does not pitch NexusPoint services as if Steven is a prospective client
Does not ask Steven to 'hop on a call' as a prospective buyer
Frames the conversation as peer-to-peer / partnership territory — e.g., asks about his build-side setup, overflow work, specialist partners, or referral dynamics
Meta-line (Phase or Tactic) flags that this is a peer/referral-partner track, not a direct-client track
Output includes Phase: and Tactic: meta-line
Contains a calibrated question that surfaces the nature of his build-side setup
